title: Payments latency rising with intermittent checkout failures
alert_summary: ALERT: payments-api latency and payment failures are rising across regions. Blast radius suggests a dependency issue.
customer_impact_summary: Customers are seeing delayed payment confirmations and repeated retries. Revenue-impacting symptoms are present in multiple regions.
root_cause: database connection pool saturation affecting payments-api
misleading_signals: recent payments-api deploy looks suspicious but is not causal

log.message: payments-api: payment timeouts exceeded threshold across regions
payments-api: upstream dependency postgres timed out during payment processing
postgres: connection pool exhausted, new queries blocked
payments-api: deploy completed 35 minutes before incident window; no error spike at deploy time

metric.summary: payments-api latency is materially above normal baseline
payment error rate is increasing alongside latency
postgres is near or at connection saturation

notes: Start from customer-visible symptoms, then validate the most likely upstream dependency before resolving.
Do not mark incidents resolved until customer impact is reduced and the suspected root cause is supported by evidence.
If postgres connection saturation is high, restarting the app tier may reduce symptoms briefly without fixing the cause.
When upstream timeouts correlate with high DB usage, inspect dependency health before blaming the nearest service.
Differentiate symptom service from root-cause service by following the failing dependency chain.
